Tool ID:
TES-A (Executable): The tool executed successfully with
 minimal effort following the authors’ original instructions.
 No modifications or non-trivial interventions were neces-
 sary beyond routine environment setup. Execution yielded
 expected outputs on target benchmarks.

TES-B (Executable with Intervention): The tool became
 executable only after moderate intervention — such as
 fixing compatibility issues, recovering missing dependen-
 cies, correcting documentation inconsistencies, or resolving
 build errors. Execution completed successfully after these
 steps.

TES-C (Partially Executable): The tool partially func-
 tioned but failed to complete full evaluation. This includes
 tools that executed only basic commands or smoke tests
 but failed on realistic benchmarks, or tools that produced
 incomplete or unreliable outputs. Substantial manual ef-
 fort may have been required, yet full functionality was not
 achieved.

TES-D (Non-Executable): The tool could not be executed
 despite best-effort attempts. This includes cases where (i)
 no source code or binary artifact was found, (ii) the tool
 failed irrecoverably during build or execution, or (iii) critical
 components were missing with no path to recovery. Tools
 in this category are considered irreproducible at the artifact
 level.

TES-E (Executed with Divergent Results): The tool exe-
 cuted and produced outputs, but the results deviated sub-
 stantially from those reported in the original paper. Such
 divergence may be quantitative (e.g., lower recall/precision)
 or qualitative (e.g., failure to detect key clone examples).
 While the tool was runnable, its behavior was not repro-
 ducible in terms of reported findings.
